items.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
items.summary: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
items.summary: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.summary: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.summary: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: Segmentation Works for OT If Operators Are Paying Attention
items.summary: Even the best segmentation strategy will fall apart without constant oversight and disciplined operations.

items.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.summary: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.summary: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.summary: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers & Businesses Alike
items.summary: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
items.summary: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyberwar Is Reshaping Everyday Life
items.summary: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyberattacks threaten hospitals, utilities, and essential services.

items.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
items.summary: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
items.summary: “Ghost-Sender" is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild.

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Burrows Into 73 Microsoft Repositories
items.summary: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasma attack on Microsoft last month.

items.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
items.summary: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

items.title: AI Slop Will Kill Cybersecurity Storytelling If We Let It
items.summary: AI-generated content threatens credibility in cybersecurity. This "Ask the Expert" column explores why human oversight matters and how to maintain authentic narratives.

items.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
items.summary: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

items.title: Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited Since Early May
items.summary: A newly discovered, critical zero-day vulnerability is under attack; a Qilin ransomware affiliate has been blamed for at least one incident.

items.title: Iran Signed a Ceasefire — Its Hackers Didn't
items.summary: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

items.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
items.summary: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.

items.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
items.summary: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
items.summary: AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.

items.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
items.summary: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.

items.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
items.summary: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
items.summary: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.summary: Organizations are growing serious about which nation's rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.summary: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.summary: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.summary: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: Cyber Insurance Rates Are Dropping, but Exclusions Widen
items.summary: Cyber insurance coverage is slowly changing, and some policies may not provide coverage for social engineering attacks like ClickFix.

items.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
items.summary: A disabled security setting meant to protect authentication across Android versions of key apps like Word, PowerPoint, and Excel paved the way for attackers to steal logins and data.

items.title: Malicious Notifications Could Trick Google Gemini Users
items.summary: A prompt injection flaw in Google Gemini's voice assistant let attackers hide malicious commands in notifications, enabling social engineering and more.

items.title: Global Stock Exchange Hit by Monthslong Email Campaign
items.summary: A threat actor got a near-continuous view into an influential finance executive's email inbox, thanks to clever use of legitimate, native Windows tools.

items.title: Zoom CISO: AI as a Security Enabler, Not Role-Replacer
items.summary: Zoom CISO Sandra McLeod discusses the challenges of securing a global communication platform, the promise of AI-driven security workflows, and her advice for aspiring cybersecurity leaders.

items.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
items.summary: Once targeting just Microsoft 365, the phishing-as-a-service platform now aims at AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms, while relying on device code phishing.

items.title: DriveSurge Hijacks Thousands of Sites for ClickFix, FakeUpdate Attacks
items.summary: A sneaky, wide-scale IAB operation uses a malicious traffic distribution system (TDS) to redirect visitors of trusted websites to ones that deliver malware.

items.title: China Uses Dual-Method Cyberattack on Czech Orgs
items.summary: China is stealing data from high-value targets via a sneaky, double-layer spear-phishing campaign that includes the Azureveil malware.

items.title: Securing AI Agents Before They Go Rogue Is Next to Impossible
items.summary: High-autonomy agents with broad permissions and unfettered access are a recipe for disaster, and enterprises need to act now before they become the next horror story.

items.title: Beyond Assume-Breach: How AI-Native Security Will Reshape Enterprise Defense
items.summary: Twenty years after Dark Reading launched, we're looking ahead at what's next for enterprise security. Spoiler: It's hyper-segmented, AI-orchestrated, and way more sophisticated than your dad's firewall.

items.title: Anthropic to Open Mythos AI to EU's ENISA
items.summary: The European security agency's entry to Project Glasswing is the result of "strong bilateral cooperation" between the European Commission and Anthropic.

items.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
items.summary: After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.summary: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.
